title: glyce maritima
intro: Glyce maritima, also known as sea buckthorn, is a small shrub-like plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, particularly its high levels of vitamin C and beta-carotene. The health benefits of sea buckthorn include its ability to boost the immune system, improve wound healing, and reduce inflammation, as well as its potential to protect against various diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Sea buckthorn is often prepared as a juice, oil, or herbal tea, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
